theme: The Destination Is Part of the Message

linkedin.text:
Most event briefs start with objectives. The smart ones start with the question: what do we want people to feel the moment they land?

After 16 years and 130+ destinations, here's what we know:

The destination is not decoration. It's a signal. It tells your participants how seriously you take the experience — before the first speaker takes the stage, before the first dinner, before any slide deck loads.

The companies we work with — HR, marketing and sales leaders — understand this. They don't choose a location because it's convenient. They choose it because it amplifies every element that comes after.

A financial firm that moved its annual leadership summit to the Azores didn't just get better weather. Their post-event survey scores on "feeling valued" jumped significantly. The location made the investment visible.

When you're evaluating destinations, here are the questions that matter:
→ Does the environment reflect the culture you're trying to build?
→ Does the logistics complexity match your team's appetite for adventure?
→ Does the destination give you assets — scenery, history, cuisine — that become part of the content?

25,000+ participants across our events have experienced what it means when the answer to those questions is yes.

Planning a destination event for 2026 or 2027? Send me a DM — happy to share what we've seen work across similar briefs, no pitch involved.

#IncentiveTravel #MICEEvents #EventProduction

instagram.text:
Your destination is doing half the work before you even open the agenda. 🌍

Croatia. Japan. Jordan. Iceland. Patagonia.

In 130+ destinations across 16 years of production, we've seen one thing consistently: when the place feels intentional, people show up differently — more present, more open, more connected to the moment and to each other.

Swipe through some of the locations that changed the room before the meeting even started. ➡️

Planning a destination event? DM us — we'll tell you what works and what to avoid.

#IncentiveTravel #MICEEvents #EventProduction

facebook.text:
The venue you choose tells your team something before the first session even starts.

A cliff-top resort in Croatia says: you earned this.
A converted warehouse in Berlin says: we think differently here.
A desert camp in Jordan says: this is unlike anything you've done before.

After producing events across 130+ destinations, we've learned that the location itself carries a message — and the best programs are built around that message, not dropped into it.

Where are you planning to take your team next? Drop it in the comments — we'd love to hear what you're considering.

image_prompt: Wide-angle photograph of a group of formally dressed corporate attendees walking across a sun-drenched ancient stone plaza in a Mediterranean coastal city, their long shadows stretching across the cobblestones, a harbor glittering in the background.
